name: Emil Kowalski
description: Emil Kowalski's motion tutorials (emilkowal.ski) defined the 2023–2026 product-motion vocabulary: springy but never bouncy, micro-interactions as information, CSS-first when possible, reduced-motion as a first-class branch. His influence reaches every serious product site from Linear to Vercel to Arc. This pack biases toward motion-forward styles while keeping the restraint that makes his motion feel designed, not animated.
rules: Springs not durations — every transition uses { bounce: 0.2, visualDuration: 0.3 } or similar Motion v12 two-parameter form.
CSS-first when the animation doesn't need state: @starting-style, animation-timeline: view(), scroll-timeline. JS motion only for interactive states.
Micro-interactions are information — a 2px lift + 50ms delay on hover is a signal, not decoration.
Reduced-motion is a first-class branch with its own thought-through states. Don't just zero the duration.
Single-accent palette. Motion carries the distinctiveness; color doesn't need to.
Typography is calm and confident. Geist or Bricolage Grotesque — never ornamental.
Motion never exceeds 600ms for UI; 400ms is the default. Over-long animations feel sluggish.
Page transitions via View Transitions API, not JS-driven route animation.
references: Linear Motion Principles (Kowalski's work at Linear)
